department_leader: Bot 7 — Food Allergy and Intolerance Assessment
key_learnings: Accurate caloric tracking requires accounting for cooking methods, food preparation losses, and individual metabolic variation. Total Daily Energy Expenditure models must be updated regularly as patient conditions change.
research_findings: Documented 8-12% variance in reported vs. actual caloric intake across self-reported dietary recall methods. Identified that TDEE calculators overestimate needs in sedentary elderly populations by up to 15%.
current_focus_areas: Developing adaptive caloric targets based on real-time metabolic feedback; improving calorie-density databases for culturally diverse food items; coordinating with Meal Planning bot on intake optimization protocols.

learning_log.entry: Assigned to the Dietary Safety & Meal Architecture department under Department Leader Bot 7 (Food Allergy and Intolerance Assessment). Continuing collaborative caloric tracking research and reporting daily to Copilot. Progress: 60%.
Progress update: Completed adaptive TDEE modeling incorporating activity variance and metabolic rate adjustments. Validated intake tracking accuracy protocols. Progress advanced to 65%.

purpose: Knowledge library resource available for all departments to reference and use
access_rule: Library access requires the diet_special_team_leader to present a case to copilot, who then presents it to starrm1. starrm1 is the final approver. Access is off by default until starrm1 approves.

access_approval_process.action: Prepares and submits a case to copilot explaining why a department needs access to the library bots
Reviews the case and presents it to starrm1 on behalf of the diet_special_team_leader
Final approver — reviews the case presented by copilot and grants or denies library access
